records.ety: [L. obvention, fr. obvenire to come before or in the way of, to befall; ob (see Ob-) + venire to come: cf. F. obvention.]

defs: The act of happening incidentally; that which happens casually; an incidental advantage; an occasional offering.
quotes: Legacies bequeathed by the deaths of princes and great persons, and other casualities and obventions.
